name: Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada
name_original: Playas y acantilados de Liencres – paseo circular por la Costa Quebrada
description: Hike the Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada, a moderate 7.0-mile route along dramatic cliffs and beaches in Cantabria, Spain.
region_intro: Hiking around Piélagos, located in Cantabria, Spain, offers diverse natural landscapes combining coastal beauty with inland terrain. The region features the Dunes of Liencres Natural Park and the Costa Quebrada, known for its extensive dune system, dramatic cliffs, natural arches, and coves. Inland, the municipality is traversed by the Pas River, with green valleys, meadows, and small mountain ranges like Monte Picota and Monte Tolío. This variety provides a range of hiking experiences across different environments. Best hiking trails around Piélagos The most popular hiking route is Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route follows the coastline, offering views of the unique geological formations of the Costa Quebrada. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monte Picota and Monte Tolío Loop, a moderate 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This trail provides inland perspectives, ascending hills and traversing varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Liencres Dunes Coastal Trail, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through the protected dune system and along beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. Hiking around Piélagos is defined by coastal paths along the Costa Quebrada, extensive dune systems, and inland routes through river valleys and small mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate ascents. The routes in Piélagos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Piélagos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Piélagos?
A: Piélagos offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 60 routes available. These range from easy coastal strolls to more moderate inland ascents, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Piélagos?
A: You'll find a diverse landscape in Piélagos. The region features dramatic coastal paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Costa Quebrada&lt;/strong&gt; with its unique geological formations, extensive dune systems like those in the &lt;strong&gt;Dunes of Liencres Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, and beautiful beaches. Inland, trails traverse green river valleys, meadows, and small mountain ranges such as Monte Picota and Monte Tolío.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Piélagos has many easy options. For a gentle coastal walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996725095/liencres-dunes-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liencres Dunes Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.0 km long and takes about 1 hour 36 minutes, leading through the protected dune system and along beaches. There are 32 easy routes in total, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Piélagos?
A: Yes, many routes in Piélagos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996734375/monte-picota-and-monte-tolio-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Picota and Monte Tolío Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers an inland perspective, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5342381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canallave Beach – Costa Quebrada Rock Formations loop&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning coastal views.

Q: What are the best trails for experiencing the coastal scenery?
A: To fully appreciate the coastal beauty, we recommend routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996723457/senda-del-litoral-costa-quebrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the coastline and showcases the unique geological formations. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrías Beach – El Madero Beach loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque views of various beaches and rock formations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Piélagos?
A: The hiking trails in Piélagos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic coastal cliffs and dunes to the serene inland river valleys, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Piélagos?
A: Many trails in Piélagos are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Dunes of Liencres Natural Park, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check local signage for specific regulations.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for?
A: Piélagos is rich in natural wonders. Along the coast, you can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/640142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Quebrada Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;, the extensive dune system of Liencres, and beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/781550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canallave Beach&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3635532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somocuevas Beach&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3637422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnía Beach&lt;/a&gt;. Inland, the Pas River and small mountains like Monte Picota offer scenic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions nearby that I can visit after a hike?
A: Yes, there are several interesting places to explore. The historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar&lt;/a&gt;, with its charming medieval architecture and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/962386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Santa Juliana&lt;/a&gt;, is a short drive away. You can also visit Santander to see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3659341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Our Lady of the Assumption&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Piélagos?
A: Piélagos is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for coastal and inland trails. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal walks where you can combine hiking with beach visits, though it can be warmer. Winters are generally mild, but some coastal paths might be exposed to stronger winds.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Piélagos?
A: While some areas of Piélagos are accessible by public transport, especially larger towns, reaching specific trailheads can be challenging. It's recommended to check local bus schedules in advance or consider driving for more flexibility to access the starting points of various routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Piélagos?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads, particularly around the Dunes of Liencres Natural Park and key coastal access points. Look for designated parking areas at beaches like Valdearenas or Canallave, and near the starting points of well-known routes. Always arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.
tips: It is a small cove with a small sandy area and surrounded by cliffs.
Liencres Beach on the Costa Quebrada Route

Bufones (blowholes) are "coastal geysers" that are predominantly seen in eastern Asturias. These are narrow openings at the end of a cave directly on the coast, which extends above the cave ceiling into the open air. If a strong wave hits the opening to the sea, it is guided through the cave walls like a funnel to the upper opening. When the surf is strong, spectacular water fountains are created, similar to those of a geyser.
When the sea is calm, all you can see are the huge holes at the top of the coast and, with a bit of luck, like at Bufon de Liencres, the opening to the sea.

The urros of Liencres will not go unnoticed before your eyes and offer a different touch to the landscape of the Cantabrian coast. These are folds and rocky ledges that protrude from the coast as if they were islands.

Arnía beach is located in the natural park of the Dunas de Liencres and Costa Quebrada, between the municipalities of Piélagos and Santa Cruz de Bezana, in the autonomous community of Cantabria, Spain. The beach is located in a rocky environment, with rocks that are more than 90 million years old.

In my opinion it is one of the most beautiful beaches I have ever visited. The tombolo only makes it accessible at low tide. The bad thing is the access by the rocks: complicated and dangerous, refrain from wearing flip flops. And be careful also with the hours of the tide.
Through the offshore island, the waves burn from 2 sides on the beach here.
Nice beach with easy access and a very nice rocky environment.
El Arenal del Jortín is a small sandy cove located next to the beach of San Juan de la Canal (Soto de la Marina) and where dogs can be taken so that they can enjoy themselves with their beach owners.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and duration of the Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. While some sections are easy and suitable for most fitness levels, the full 11.2 km route involves gentle climbs and descents, requiring good fitness. It typically takes around 3 hours to complete.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain. You'll walk on paths (about 66%), forested or wild trails (15%), dirt roads (8.5%), and some asphalt sections (7.7%). The path generally runs above the cliffs, with gentle ascents and descents. Sure-footedness is recommended, especially near coastal viewpoints.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail is renowned for its dramatic coastal scenery and unique geological formations. You'll encounter impressive cliffs, sea stacks known as 'Urros' (like the Urros of Liencres), and numerous beautiful beaches such as Cerrías Beach, Portio Beach, Arnía Beach, and Playa de Covachos. Don't miss the Liencres Blowhole and the Covachos Tombolo, which is accessible at low tide. There are also several viewpoints offering panoramic sea views, including View of Covachos Beach and Alto de Casuca.

faqs.question: Is the Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y Costa Quebrada, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially near cliff edges and on beaches where regulations might apply during certain seasons.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada. It is located within the Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y Costa Quebrada, which is freely accessible to the public. However, always respect natural park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing flora or fauna.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Costa Quebrada?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically during spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is pleasant, and the crowds are smaller. Summer can be beautiful but also busier, especially on the beaches. Winters are generally mild but can bring more rain and stronger winds, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The Senda del Litoral: Costa Quebrada spans a significant coastline. For this specific Komoot tour, which is a circular walk, you can typically find parking near the starting point in Liencres or nearby beaches like Arnía or Covachos. Public transport options might be available to connect to various points along the broader Senda del Litoral, but for this specific circular route, driving and parking at a convenient spot is often the easiest.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows sections of the official Senda del Litoral (60%). It also passes through smaller local paths such as Barrio Las Cerreas (7%), Urbanización La Canal (5%), Barrio La Arnía (5%), Barrio Llatas (4%), Barrio Cerrías (3%), and Urbanización Universidad (2%).

faqs.question: What makes the Costa Quebrada area geologically significant?
faqs.answer: The Costa Quebrada is considered an internationally recognized geological treasure, often called a 'geological outdoor classroom.' It showcases a unique variety of geological features in a small area, including cliffs, arches, islets, tombolos, and dunes, which tell a geological history spanning millions of years. It's even aspiring to be part of the UNESCO Global Geoparks network.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, you will find amenities in the villages and near popular beaches along the route. Areas around Liencres, Arnía, and San Juan de la Canal often have cafes, restaurants, and sometimes public restrooms, especially during peak season. It's always a good idea to carry water and snacks, as facilities might not be continuously available.
